file_name: ADMA BioManufacturing, LLC -  Amendment #3 to Manufacturing Agreement .txt

perturbation.original_text: All claims by Sanofi Pasteur for breach or default under this Agreement shall be brought within [***] year after the cause of action comes into existence or otherwise shall be waived.
perturbation.changed_text: All claims by Sanofi Pasteur for breach or default under this Agreement shall be brought within 6 months after the cause of action comes into existence or otherwise shall be waived.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the claim period to 6 months may conflict with Florida's statute of limitations for breach of contract.
perturbation.law_explanation: Florida Statute § 95.11(2)(b) specifies a five-year statute of limitations for actions founded on a written contract. Reducing the claim period to 6 months in the contract directly contradicts this statute by shortening the legally permissible time frame to bring a breach of contract claim.
perturbation.justification: The original contract specifies a claim period of '[***] year' while the changed text shortens this period to '6 months'. The Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation both indicate this reduction may conflict with Florida's statute of limitations, which is five years for written contracts. This creates an inconsistency where the contractually agreed-upon claim period is significantly shorter than the legally permissible timeframe. The Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction definition directly applies, as the changed text sets a deadline violating state law.

perturbation.original_text: Shipment of the Source Plasma to Sanofi Pasteur shall be made in compliance with the transportation conditions as provided in the Quality Agreement (as defined below), to be further amended by the Parties as contemplated in Section 9 below.
perturbation.changed_text: Shipment of the Source Plasma to Sanofi Pasteur shall be made without specific adherence to transportation regulations, relying solely on internal company guidelines. The Quality Agreement referenced below will not be amended.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the requirement to comply with transportation regulations may violate federal and state laws regarding the shipment of biological materials.
perturbation.law_explanation: The changed text explicitly states that shipments will be made without adherence to transportation regulations, potentially violating 49 CFR § 171-180, which covers hazardous materials regulations. Since source plasma is derived from human blood, it will fall under hazardous material regulations because of its potential to transmit diseases. Therefore, failure to comply is a violation of the cited law.

perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that shipments will be made without adherence to transportation regulations, which directly contradicts the purpose of 49 CFR Part 171, as highlighted in the scraped snippets. This regulation, according to the snippets, pertains to general information, regulations, and definitions related to transportation, suggesting a comprehensive framework for safe and compliant shipping practices. The modified contract's deviation from these regulations establishes a clear contradiction, as it removes a legal obligation that is designed to ensure safety and compliance.
